mission.title: linkerd: Customize mirrored service name
mission.description: Customize mirrored service name. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
### What problem are you trying to solve?

I'd like to control how the local service name (in the source cluster) of a mirrored service is being generated.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Customize mirrored service name
steps.description:
Hi , thanks for opening this.  It makes total sense to want to ability to customize the mirror service name so that clients can be agnostic to which cluster the exported service is running in.  I'm not sure that passing in a go template for the name is the right approach, however.

One advantage of the TrafficSplit based approach you mentioned above is that it gives you more flexibility and control over where the traffic is sent.  For example, if you have both `api-west` and `api-south` mirror

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Customize mirrored service name" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Hi , thanks for opening this.  It makes total sense to want to ability to customize the mirror service name so that clients can be agnostic to which cluster the exported service is running in.  I'm not sure that passing in a go template for the name is the right approach, however.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
